title: The Window Closes at Twenty. PFAS May Already Be Stealing What Your Daughter Can Never Get Back.
description: Peak bone mass is built once — in childhood and adolescence — and then it's gone forever. A landmark 2026 study confirms that PFAS, detectable in 97% of Americans, measurably reduce bone density during this irreversible window, with girls bearing a disproportionate burden. The fractures and osteoporosis that follow may be the slow-motion public health catastrophe nobody is talking about.
heroImageAlt: Editorial illustration for The Window Closes at Twenty. PFAS May Already Be Stealing What Your Daughter Can Never Get Back.
